description: Master registry of known data anomalies and expected exceptions across all five products and 30 countries. Two purposes: (1) INTERNAL reference so the same expected exception is never re-investigated as a defect; (2) SOURCE for the client-facing anomaly disclosures attached to each quality report at delivery. Entries with client_facing=true are surfaced to clients so they can account for them during backtesting and model training; entries with client_facing=false are internal-only.

categories.DATA_GAP: A missing observation period for a documented, permanent reason (government lapse, discontinuation).
categories.VALUE_EXTREME: A genuine but unusually large/small value that can trip naive range checks; NOT an error.
categories.PATH_CONVENTION: A cosmetic partition-path formatting inconsistency; does not affect data content.
categories.FILL_ROWS: Interpolated/carried-forward rows (is_interpolated=true) used to keep monthly series gap-free.
categories.COVERAGE_CHANGE: A change to which countries/series are in scope.
categories.PIPELINE_ARTIFACT: A bookkeeping/ingestion field that is not a publication or PIT event; do not use for backtesting gates.
categories.FILENAME_HISTORY: A historical vault filenaming issue, recorded for provenance; typically internal-only.
categories.EXPECTED_METRIC_BEHAVIOR: A value pattern (sign, magnitude, or ratio) that is economically normal for the metric type but would trip a naive automated sanity check that doesn't know the metric's semantics.

anomalies.title: October 2025 US federal appropriations lapse — BLS data unavailable
anomalies.description: For the October 2025 reference month, all affected BLS series returned '-' from the public API (footnote codes 9 or X). The US federal government operated without appropriations, so BLS could not collect or publish that month.
anomalies.reason: US government shutdown / lapse in appropriations, October 2025.
affected_series: and all other BLS price/employment series
anomalies.client_guidance: Treat October 2025 as legitimately missing for affected BLS series (do NOT interpret as a scraper error). BLS does not retroactively publish lapse-period data. CES0500000003 (avg hourly earnings) was NOT affected and has a real Oct-2025 value (36.85). November 2025 resumed normally. For gap-free models, forward-fill October 2025 from September 2025 for the affected series only.
anomalies.internal_notes: Confirmed 2026-06-25 via BLS API. See configs/catalog_expected_series.yaml known_gaps (gap_id BLS_2025_APPROPRIATIONS_LAPSE) for the authoritative list + vault actions taken. Temporal-consistency stages consult KNOWN_GAP_MONTHS={'2025-10'} so they no longer FAIL on it.

anomalies.title: Building-permit indices (2015=100) legitimately reach ~9300 for small member states
anomalies.description: The Residential Building Permits index (Index 2015=100, NSA, quarterly) reaches values up to ~9312 for small EU member states. This is genuine: these countries had tiny, volatile permit counts in the 2015 base year, so later readings are 20x-90x the base.
anomalies.reason: Small/volatile 2015 base-year permit counts inflate a 2015=100 index; NSA quarterly permit data is inherently spiky.
anomalies.client_guidance: These are real index values, not outliers to discard. Range checks on this metric should allow up to ~20000. Consider modeling this permit index in log space or as period-over-period change rather than levels, given the wide dynamic range across member states.
anomalies.internal_notes: Observed max 9312.0 across 70 rows. GX observed_value cap widened 2000 -> 20000 in configs/gx_config_housing_eu27_vault.json to stop false GX failures.

anomalies.title: Some Hive month= partition folders are not zero-padded (month=1 vs month=01)
anomalies.description: A subset of partition folders for non-USA countries use non-zero-padded month names (e.g. month=1 instead of month=01). This is a filesystem path-formatting inconsistency only; the data inside is correct and the observation dates in the records are unaffected.
anomalies.reason: Early partition writes did not zero-pad the month path segment.
anomalies.client_guidance: When enumerating partitions by path, parse the month as an integer rather than a fixed-width string. Reading the parquet content directly (reporting_date / data_timestamp) is unaffected and is the recommended time index.
anomalies.internal_notes: Surfaced as MEDIUM PATH_CONVENTION findings in the quality report. Cosmetic; a repartition pass could normalize it but is not required for delivery.

anomalies.title: Interpolated fill rows (is_interpolated=true) keep monthly series gap-free
anomalies.description: Some EU27 series are natively quarterly or have publication gaps; to deliver a continuous monthly grid, interpolated/carried-forward rows are inserted and explicitly flagged with is_interpolated=true.
anomalies.reason: Source series are lower-frequency or intermittently published; monthly continuity is provided via flagged fill rows.
anomalies.client_guidance: Filter on is_interpolated=false if your model must use only originally-published observations. The is_interpolated flag is authoritative — never infer fill status from value smoothness.
anomalies.internal_notes: Surfaced as INFO EXPECTED_FILL findings in the quality report (e.g. trade_flows/EU27 ~66.7%, wages/EU27 ~18.2%). is_interpolated is a Eurostat-only column (absent on USA/GBR/CAN partitions).

anomalies.title: Australia and Norway decommissioned — coverage is now 30 countries
anomalies.description: Australia (AUS) and Norway (NOR) were removed from all products on 2026-07-10. Vault coverage is now 30 sovereign jurisdictions: USA + EU27 + GBR + CAN. Any older artifact referencing 32 countries or AUS/NOR predates this change.
anomalies.client_guidance: Expect 30 countries, not 32. Do not request or expect AUS or NOR series in any product.
anomalies.internal_notes: Historical dated quality-report snapshots (pre 2026-07-10) still say 32 / list AUS-NOR by design (point-in-time record). Live/current reports say 30.

anomalies.title: conversion_timestamp is pipeline bookkeeping, not a publication/PIT event
anomalies.description: The conversion_timestamp column exists only on the food_micropricing/USA and wages_and_employment/USA partitions. It records when the ingestion pipeline last wrote the record to disk — it is NOT a government publication date and NOT a point-in-time event.
anomalies.reason: ETL bookkeeping field materialized only by the USA ingestion path.
anomalies.client_guidance: Never use conversion_timestamp as a PIT gate for backtesting. Use published_date / official_release_date. conversion_timestamp is absent on all EU27/GBR/CAN partitions and on USA housing/trade/global_macro.
anomalies.internal_notes: Documented in the per-product data dictionaries (Provenance Fields section).

anomalies.title: as_of_date set to pipeline run date (not publication date) in some sample records
anomalies.description: In the Wages, Housing, and Trade SAMPLE parquets, as_of_date was set to the pipeline run date (2026-06-19T10:00:00Z) rather than the original publication date. Production vault records reflect the intended knowledge cutoff.
anomalies.client_guidance: Always use published_date / official_release_date as the PIT gate — never as_of_date or conversion_timestamp. Documented in the per-product changelogs.
anomalies.internal_notes: Known inconsistency noted in the data dictionaries.

anomalies.title: USA housing vault files were briefly named with EU-flavored stems (resolved)
anomalies.description: A content-based renaming utility (scrapers/eurostat/audit_rename_vault_filenames.py) mislabeled USA housing files with EU-flavored stems: US Census permits as permits_eu27_data.parquet and US BLS CPI shelter as housing_hicp_rent_data.parquet. Content was always genuine USA (iso=USA, agencies BLS/CENSUS). Files renamed to shelter_inflation_data.parquet / housing_permits_data.parquet on 2026-07-11 and the tool now skips non-EU countries.
anomalies.reason: EU27 naming tool rglob'd all countries without a country guard.
anomalies.client_guidance: No client action. Filenames are internal vault structure and are not part of the delivered schema.
anomalies.internal_notes: 1,604 files renamed (808 shelter + 796 permits). Root cause fixed in audit_rename_vault_filenames.py (_NON_EU_COUNTRIES guard). Validators glob *_data.parquet so they are filename-robust regardless.

anomalies.title: Trade-balance, current-account, and fiscal-balance metrics are net/signed measures and legitimately go negative
anomalies.description: A full sweep of all 30 countries confirmed negative values in series such as TRADE_BAL_* (trade_flows, e.g. CAN, GBR) and BOPGSTB (USA goods trade balance, observed range -132,983 to -831 USD_MILLIONS), plus BCA_NGDPD (current account % of GDP) and GGXCNL_NGDP (general government net lending/borrowing % of GDP) in global_macro. These are net/signed measures by definition — a trade deficit, current-account deficit, or fiscal deficit is a real, common economic state, not a data error.
anomalies.reason: These metrics measure a net flow or balance (exports minus imports, revenue minus spend), which is negative whenever the country runs a deficit.
anomalies.client_guidance: Do not flag negative values in balance/net-type series as data errors in your own QA. If you run automated sanity checks, allow negative ranges for any series whose name/metric implies a balance, deficit, surplus, or net position.
anomalies.internal_notes: Confirmed via a systematic 150-combination (5 products x 30 countries) sweep on 2026-07-11. Also confirmed HICP_ALL_YOY (YoY inflation rate) goes negative in 12 EU countries during deflation periods — same principle, rate-type metric, not listed as a separate series here since it is covered by the general 'rate/balance metrics can be negative' guidance.

anomalies.title: HICP year-over-year inflation rate goes negative during deflation periods
anomalies.description: HICP_ALL_YOY (EU27 harmonized CPI, year-over-year % change) shows negative values for multiple member states (confirmed in BEL, BGR, CYP, DNK, ESP, GRC, IRL, LUX, LVA, NLD, PRT, SVN during the sampled window). A negative YoY reading means prices fell versus a year earlier (deflation) — a real, if less common, macroeconomic condition, not a data defect.
anomalies.reason: Deflationary periods are real; a rate-of-change metric is expected to be negative when they occur.
anomalies.client_guidance: Treat negative HICP_ALL_YOY values as genuine deflation readings, not errors. Do not clip or discard negative values before analysis unless your model specifically requires inflation-only regimes.

anomalies.title: IMF WEO forecast years (2027-2031) are annual observations, not monthly — apparent monthly gaps are not real
anomalies.description: For forecast years beyond the ALFRED historical window, global_macro/USA switches to source=imf_weo, which publishes one annual observation per forecast year (e.g. a single 2027-01-01 dated row per series, not one row per month). A naive monthly-continuity check on this window will report ~11 'missing months' per forecast year; this is expected — IMF WEO forecasts are inherently annual.
anomalies.reason: IMF World Economic Outlook publishes annual forecast figures, not monthly.
affected_series: all imf_weo-sourced series in global_macro/USA for years 2027 and later
anomalies.client_guidance: Do not run monthly gap-continuity checks on the IMF WEO forecast window without first checking the `source` column — filter to source=imf_weo and expect annual cadence there, vs monthly for source=alfred_vintage historical data.
anomalies.internal_notes: Confirmed via the 2026-07-11 systematic sweep, which initially flagged this as a 44-month GAP finding before checking the source column.

client_reported.description: Anomalies flagged by clients AFTER delivery. Append here as they are reported so future deliveries and support can reference them. Each entry mirrors the anomalies[] schema plus reported_by and reported_date.
